text: Data visualization is a cornerstone of scientific communication, yet many researchers are hindered by the steep learning curves of programming-based tools or the high licensing costs of commercial software. To bridge this gap, we developed SRplot (Scientific and Research plot tool), a comprehensive, free online platform designed for intuitive data visualization and graphing. SRplot integrates over 120 specialized modules tailored for biomedical and bioinformatics research, including tools for genomics, transcriptomics, clinical data, and network analysis. The platform features a user-friendly graphical interface that allows researchers to generate high-quality plots in real-time by simply pasting their datasets. Key advantages include server-side computation, which eliminates the need for high-performance local hardware, and the inclusion of built-in biological databases such as GO, KEGG, and TCGA. Users can extensively customize graphical parameters and export publication-ready figures in both bitmap (PNG, TIFF) and vector (PDF, SVG) formats. Since its inception, SRplot has supported more than 46,000 users and has been cited in over 550 peer-reviewed journals. SRplot is continuously updated based on community feedback and is freely accessible at http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot.
